sourceMovie.title: Still Alice
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Still Alice works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: A Beautiful Mind
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The drama appeal of Still Alice — that bittersweet edge, the romance — translates cleanly into A Beautiful Mind (2001). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: What's Eating Gilbert Grape
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind What's Eating Gilbert Grape (1993) clearly understood what makes drama work — the same quality that keeps Still Alice rewatchable. Expect the same bittersweet rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: The Father
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Father (2020) is the kind of film Still Alice fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The drama overlap is real, but it's the tense emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: A Man Called Otto
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: A Man Called Otto shares Still Alice's gift for drama — the 2022 film delivers comedy without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Fathers and Daughters
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Still Alice compelling — the drama, the atmosphere — shows up in Fathers and Daughters (2015) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that tense storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Through My Window: Across the Sea
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The drama energy of Still Alice is alive in Through My Window: Across the Sea (2023), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a funny payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: The Glass Castle
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Glass Castle (2017) operates in the same drama as Still Alice, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The tense payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: The Man Who Invented Christmas
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Man Who Invented Christmas (2017) belongs to the same conversation as Still Alice — both anchor their drama in history emotion that doesn't let go. The funny undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: You're Not You
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Still Alice's drama stayed with you, You're Not You (2014) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The tense through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Proof
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Still Alice excels in drama, Proof (2005) doubles down on mystery to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

faq.question: What should I watch after Still Alice?
faq.answer: Try A Beautiful Mind, What's Eating Gilbert Grape, The Father—each shares DNA with Still Alice while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Still Alice?
faq.answer: Still Alice (2014) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Still Alice worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
